---
applyTo: "{packages/web-platform/web-core/**,packages/web-platform/web-core-e2e/**,packages/react/**/element-template/**,packages/webpack/react-webpack-plugin/**,packages/rspeedy/lynx-bundle-rslib-config/**}"
---

ReactLynx element templates are compile-time assets collected by the React webpack plugin and passed to web-core as `encodeData.elementTemplates`. The web binary encoder may normalize a collected template record into the persisted asset array, but the decoded `ElementTemplates` section should use only that array shape. For web-core CSR support, emit/register the binary `ElementTemplates` section before `LepusCode`, and include builtins such as `_et_builtin_raw_text` in the asset array rather than synthesizing them in Rust. Avoid Rust repeatedly reflecting over the compiled-template JS object: TypeScript should traverse decoded assets once and call the Rust `ElementTemplateDefinitionBuilder` update methods, while Rust/WASM keeps template DOM instantiation, unique-id registration, class/style updates, CSSOG refreshes, event registration, slot insert/remove, and serialization. Use inert HTML `<template>` elements for compiled definitions and invisible anchors for element slots so normal child traversal does not include placeholder nodes.
For `ElementTemplateDefinitionBuilder`, keep `elements` as the temporary build-time index table that maps TypeScript traversal indexes to inert template nodes; do not move DOM creation to TypeScript just to remove that table, because Rust would still need equivalent node references while losing the centralized tag/attribute/style logic. Avoid storing builder fields that can be derived from the template itself, such as the owner document.
For client inline style and element-template style handling, keep `transformVW`/`transformVH`/`transformREM` as `MainThreadWasmContext` state after construction; do not re-pass those transform flags through per-call style APIs or duplicate them on element-template builders.
For web-core client unique IDs, ordinary TS-created elements should set the JS `uniqueIdSymbol` in TypeScript after Rust returns the id. Rust-created element-template nodes should not call back into the JS binding to set that symbol; store the id on a private JS property from Rust and keep `__GetElementUniqueID` as the single reader that checks symbol/property/attribute forms.
When building template definitions through custom elements, setting `text` on `x-text` or `raw-text` can run web-elements' `RawTextAttributes` handler and append a text node before the HTML template is cloned. Clear that construction-time side effect in the template definition; the cloned runtime instance should be the only place where the `text` attribute materializes text content.
